scene_input.scene_summary: A Muslim woman is shopping at the Qaynuqāʿ market.
scene_input.key_visual_action: Muslim woman holding a basket of goods, smiling at a vendor.

schema_output.scene_title: A Joyful Encounter at the Market
schema_output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, p. 158
schema_output.moral_lesson: The joy of community and commerce in Islam

characters.name: Muslim woman
characters.appearance: medium build, dignified upright posture, wearing a long-sleeved white jilbab with a loose-fitting white khimar covering her hair, a pair of worn leather sandals on her feet, and carrying a woven basket with a colorful pattern

schema_output.narrative_text: In the bustling Qaynuqāʿ market, a Muslim woman finds joy in shopping for fresh produce and goods, interacting with friendly vendors and building connections with the community.
schema_output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor storybook illustration, soft warm hand-painted style for ages 6-12. A lively 7th century Makkah market scene with a packed earth floor and wooden stalls under a canopy of intricately woven palm-fiber mats. Late afternoon sunlight casts a warm glow over the scene, with the sun shining through the gaps between the mat's wooden pillars. In the foreground, the central figure stands with her back to the viewer, wearing a long-sleeved white jilbab and khimar, her hands grasping a woven basket filled with colorful fruits and vegetables. She smiles warmly as she converses with a vendor, a middle-aged man with a kind face, seen from the side, wearing a simple white thobe and a pair of worn leather slippers. The vendor gestures with one hand, pointing to a selection of goods on display. Soft warm golden light, lively and vibrant mood. Medium shot at eye level. Warm sand, cream, and golden palette with the vendor's white thobe as a color anchor. No modern objects, historically accurate 7th century scene.
